The International (2009)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $25,450,527Details
International Box Office $36,155,385
Worldwide Box Office $61,605,912
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $8,015,247
Total $8,015,247
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Interpol agent Louis Salinger joins forces with New York prosecutor Eleanor Whitman to put an end to a powerful bank’s funding of terrorism. As they follow the money from Germany to Italy to New York to Turkey, Salinger and Whitman find their own lives are at risk from those who will stop at nothing to protect their interests.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$9,331,739 (36.7% of total gross)
Legs:2.73 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:41.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$50,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,364 opening theaters/2,364 max. theaters, 3.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $40,585,112

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 13th, 2009 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
Video Release: June 9th, 2009 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for some sequences of violence and language.
Running Time: 118 minutes
Plot point: Corporate Malfeasance, Corrupt Cops, Government Corruption, Interpol, Political, Political Assassination, Revenge
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, Relativity Media, Atlas Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
